did.abstract: Cornell song books and sheet music.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Cornell song books. Booklets, and sheet music and other music and lyric sheets. A number of the songs are written by Cornell alumni and were performed by the Cornell Glee Club. Some of the items contain history or other information about the songs and their composers. Some tunes are Ithaca or locally related and others are not Cornell related, but standard college and university items. Includes, but not limited to: "Big Red Team," by Romeyn Berry, 1905, with picture of football team; "Carnelian and White," by K. L. Roberts and T. J. Lindorff, 1906; "Fight for Cornell," by K. L. Roberts and T. J. Lindorff, 1907; "We March to Victory," by G. F. Pond, 1908; "Cornell Victorious," by S. H. Ayer, 1915, with picture of Touchdown, the bear mascot; "My Old Cornell," by Will A. Dillon, n.d; Cornell drinking, Glee Club and sporting event songs, and others. Also some band music and musical program from the opening of Sage Chapel, June 13, 1875; Banquet Songs of XCI (Class of 1891?), February 15, 1889. There are multiple copies of some items.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Cornell University Glee Club Records, #37-6-2399, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMA02399.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cover features what appears to be Roberts and Stone Halls. Includes: Alma Mater, Cornell, The Song Of The Classes, Evening Song At Cornell, The Chimes, The Call Of The Fields, Bustonian Chorus, There's A Wedding In The Orchard, In The Gloaming, The Old Farm At Cornell, Last Night, I Arise From Dreams Of Thee, Drink To Me Only With Thine Eyes, Juanita, The Quilting Party, Jingle, BElls, When First I Kissed Sweet Margaret, Sweet And Low, Annie Laurie, Here's To The Girl That I Love, Forsaken, The Loreley, Flow Gently, Sweet Afton, Jolly Boating Weather, Sailing, Soldier's Farewell, Nancy Lee, Old Black Joe, Old Folks At Home, My Bonnie, Down In Mobile, Nelly Was A Lady, The Two Roses, Darling Nelly Gray, How Can I Leave Thee!, Hard Times Come Again No More, Landlord, Fill The Flowing Bowl, Upidee, The Man Who Has Plenty Of Good Peanuts, Solomon Levi, The Pope, The Old Oaken Bucket, Home Again, Home, Sweet Home, Lead, Kindly Night, Rocked In The Cradle Of The Deep, The Star-Spangled Banner, Stars Of The Summer Night, The Red, White, And Blue, America, Battle Hymn Of The Republic, John Brown's Body, Maryland! My Maryland!, Dixie's Land, We're Tenting To-Night, The Watch On The Rhine, The Marseillaise, Round The Old Camp Fire, Old Nassau, Sans Souci, Our College Cheer, Auld Lang Syne, Good-Night, Ladies!.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented by the Sphinx Head Senior Honorary Society. Also includes a bit of history about each song. Includes Alma Mater by Archibald Croswell Weeks '72 and Wilmot Moses Smith '74. The words of the Cornell Alma Mater were written early in 1872, through mutual suggestion, by Weeks and Smith, who then occupied rooms in the old Woodruff block on Tioga Street. Cornell Victorious by S. Hibbard Ayer Jr. '14. Cornell, lyrics by George Kingsley Birge '72 to the tune 'Twas My Last Cigar. First published in the Cornell Era spring 1869.Song of the Classes by Frank A. Abbott '90. Give My Regards to Davy to the tune of Give My Regards to Broadway. Crew Song, lyrics by Robert James Kellog '91 and music by William Luton Wood. First published in the 1892 Cornellian. Alumni Song, lyrics by Louis Carl Ehle '90, music by Wiliam Luton Wood. First published in the 1893 Cornellian. Evening Song, lrycs by Henry Tyrrell '80, to the tune Tannenbaum. First published in the Cornell Era 1877.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various songs popular at colleges at the time, not Cornell specific. O Alma Mater, Aura Lee, Bavarian Yodel, Ching A Ling, Camptown Races, Emmett's Lullaby, Fill Up Your Stein, Forty Nine bottles, Go Down Moses, Good Night Ladies, Home Sweet Home, It's A Way We Have At Old Harvard, Lauriger Horatius, Little Brown Jug, A Little More Cider, My Meerschaum Pipe, Mary Had A Little Lamb, My Last Cigar, Mary And Martha, Old King Cole, Old Noah He Did Build An Ark, The Pope, Peter Gray, Rocked In The Cradle Of The Deep, Rio Grande, Swing Low Sweet Chariot, Tom Big Bee River, There Were Three Crows, Turn Back Pharoh's Army, There's Music In The Air, Three Little Pigs, Where Oh Where Is My Little Dog Gone, Where Oh Where, Where Are You Going My Pretty Maid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cover features Roberts and Stone Halls. Published by Hinds, Noble & Eldredge. Includes foreword by Liberty Hyde Bailey. Includes Cornell Songs: All Round The World, Alma Mater, The Big Red Team or The Foot-Ball Song, Bustonian Chorus, The Call Of The Fields, Carnelian And White, The Chimes, Cornell, Dreaming, 1875: The Cornell Cheer, Evening Song, Fight For Cornell, Isle Of Delight, Locomotive Yell, The Old Farm At Cornell, Short Yell, The Song Of The Classes, Yells. Songs of Other Colleges: Alma Mater – Amherst, Alma Mater – University of Chicago, Cheer For Old Amherst, Dear Old Yale, Fair Harvard, Hail, Pennsylvania!, Old Nassau, Orange And The Black, Our College Cheer, Sans Souci, We Stand By Our Classes, The Yellow And The Blue. Humorous Songs: Broke! Broke! Broke!, The Bull-Dog, Change of the Short Ags, Doctor Peter Price's Permanent Panacea, The Dutch Company, He Was Nervous, I Wish You'd Shave, Nut Brown Maiden, A Parody Song, Poor Ned, Where, O Where?. Popular College Songs: Am I Not Fondly Thine Own?, Annie Laurie, Auld Lang Syne, Ben Bolt, A College Medley, Down In Mobile, Drinking Song, Drink To Me Only With Thine Eyes, Forsaken, Good-night, Ladies!, Here's To The Girl That I Love, Home, Sweet Home, How Can I Leave Thee?, I Arise From The Dreams Of Thee, Jolly Boating Weather, Juanita, Last Night, Love's Old Sweet Song, Maid Of Athens, Massa's In De Cold Ground, My Bonnie, My Old Kentucky Home, Nelly Was A Lady, Old Folks At Home, The Old Oaken Bucket, Over The Banister, The Quilting Party, Romeo And Juliet, Soldier's Farewell, Stars Of The Summer Night, Sweet And Low, Sweetheart, I Wait For Thee, Take My Love To Rosalie, There's Music In The Aid, The Two Roses, WE Meet Again To-night. Patriot And Sacred Songs: America, Battle Hymn of The Republic, Dixie;s Land, Jerusalem The Golden, John Brown's Body, Lead, Kindly Light, The Marseillaise, Maryland!, My Maryland!, Old Black Joe, The Red White, and Blue, Russian Hymn, The Star-Spangled Banner, The Watch On The Rhine, We're Tenting To-night.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same content as 1915 edition, except it omits Jingle Bells and On the Chapel Steps; adds Cornell Victorious, Old Man Noah, and Dartmouth, Our Dartmouth; and substitutes Dear Old Yale for Down the Field. Published and compiled by the Board of Editors at the authorization of the Agricultural Association of the New York State College of Agriculture at Cornell University. Published by Hinds, Hayden, & Eldredge. No foreword; includes preface by Leonard C. Treman '14 and Thad. C. Logan '16, followed by photographs of architects' sketches for "the new residence halls" (on West Campus) and "the new armory" (Barton Hall). Includes: Alma Mater; All 'Round the World, Cornell; Alumni Song, And When I Die, At Eight Upon the Hill, Auld Lang Syne, Australia, The Big Red Team or The Foot-Ball Song; Blow Ye Winds, Heigh-ho!; Boating Song; Bring the Wagon Home, John; Bustonian Chorus; By the Watermelon Vine, Lindy Lou; Carnelian And White, Carry Me Back To Old Virginny, Castle On The Nile, The Chimes, Cheer For the Team, Co-Eds' Chant, Cornell, Cornell Hymn, Cornellio, Cornell Victorious, Cornell Yells, Crew Song, Dear Old Pals, Dixie's Land, Down By the Stream (Rebecca), Down In Mobile, Dreaming, Drink To Me Only With Thine Eyes, 1875-The Cornell Cheer, Eli, Evening Song, Everybody Works But Davy, Faculty, Fight For Cornell, Forty-Nine Bottles, Founder's Centenary Hymn, Founder's Hymn, Germany-Land, The Girls of Ithaca, Give My Regards To Davy, Glorious Mother-Cornell; Good-night, Ladies!; H-e-r-p-i-c-i-d-e; Honey, Dat I Love So Well; The Isle Of Delight, Juanita, The King Of the Cannibal Islands, Last Night, Levee Song, The Little Old Red Shawl, The Midshipmite; Moon, Moon; My Old Kentucky Home, Old Black Joe, Old College Chum, Old Folks At Home, Old Man Noah; One, Two, Three, Four; The Poughkeepsie Races, Rocked In the Cradle Of the Deep, Rowing Song, Sage Maidens, Santa Lucia; Senior, Junior, Soph., Frosh.; Soldier's Farewell, The Song Of the Classes, The Spanish Cavalier, Stars Of the Summer Night; Sweetheart, I Wait For Thee; Tell Me, Maiden; Upon the College Campus, Campus Song (University of Rochester), Cheer For Old Amherst; Dartmouth, Our Dartmouth; Dear Old Yale, Fair Harvard, The Genesee (University of Rochester), Hail! Pennsylvania, Hamilton's Song; Oh, Syracuse; Old Nassau (Princeton), The Orange and the Black (Princeton), Our Director (Harvard), The Princeton Cannon Song March, The Royal Purple (Williams), Yale Boola Song, The Yellow and the Blue (Michigan).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same content as 1921 edition, except that it adds Cornell Will Shine Tonight; Give Us a Drink, Bartender; Hail Thou in Majesty Cornell, The Hill, I'm a Little Prairie Flower, My Bonnie, and Row Your Boat; omits Australia, Founder's Centenary Hymn, Founder's Hymn, The Girls of Ithaca, The King of the Cannibal Islands, Last Night, The Midshipmite; Moon, Moon; Sage Maidens; Sweetheart, I Wait For Thee, and Upon the College Campus; and substitutes Annie Laurie for At Eight Upon the Hill, and Love's Old Sweet Song for The Little Old Red Shawl. Published and compiled by the Board of Editors at the authorization of the Agricultural Association of the New York State College of Agriculture at Cornell University. Published by Hinds, Hayden, & Eldredge. No foreword; includes preface by Leonard C. Treman '14 and Thad. C. Logan '16, followed by photographs of Willard Straight Hall and "Cornell dormitories" (North Campus). Includes: Alma Mater; All 'Round the World, Cornell; Alumni Song, And When I Die, Annie Laurie, Auld Lang Syne; The Big Red Team, or The Foot-Ball Song; Blow Ye Winds, Heigh-ho!; Boating Song; Bring the Wagon Home, John; Bustonian Chorus; By the Watermelon Vine, Lindy Lou; Carnelian And White, Carry Me Back To Old Virginny, Castle On the Nile, Cheer For the Team, The Chimes, Co-Eds' Chant, Cornell, Cornell Hymn, Cornellio, Cornell Victorious, Cornell Will Shine Tonight, Cornell Yells, Crew Song, Dear Old Pals, Dixie's Land, Down By the Stream (Rebecca), Down In Mobile, Dreaming, Drink To Me Only With Thine Eyes, 1875-The Cornell Cheer, Eli, Evening Song, Everybody Works But Davy, Faculty, Fight For Cornell, Forty-Nine Bottles, Germany-Land, Give My Regards To Davy; Give Us a Drink, Bartender; Glorious Mother-Cornell; Good-night, Ladies!; Hail Thou in Majesty Cornell; H-e-r-p-i-c-i-d-e; The Hill; Honey, Dat I Love So Well; I'm a Little Prairie Flower, The Isle Of Delight, Juanita, Levee Song, Love's Old Sweet Song, My Bonnie, My Old Kentucky Home, Old Black Joe, Old College Chum, Old Folks At Home, Old Man Noah; One, Two, Three, Four; The Poughkeepsie Races, Rocked In the Cradle of the Deep, Rowing Song, Row Your Boat, Santa Lucia; Senior, Junior, Soph., Frosh.; Soldier's Farewell, The Song Of the Classes, The Spanish Cavalier, Stars Of the Summer Night; Tell Me, Maiden; Campus Song (University of Rochester), Cheer For Old Amherst; Dartmouth, Our Dartmouth; Dear Old Yale, Fair Harvard, The Genesee (University of Rochester), Hail! Pennsylvania, Hamilton's Song; Oh, Syracuse; Old Nassau (Princeton), The Orange and the Black (Princeton), Our Director (Harvard), The Princeton Cannon Song March, The Royal Purple (Williams), Yale Boola Song, The Yellow and the Blue (Michigan).

scopecontent.p: Compiled and Edited by Paul J. Weaver, published by the Cornell Alumni Association. Thomas Sokol's copy, with his signature, markings for conducting the music, and extensive notes in the index.Includes: All 'Round the World, Cornell; Alma Mater, Alumnae Song, Alumni Song, The Big Red Team, Boating Song, Bustonian Chorus, Carnelian and White, The Changes (also listed as Jennie McGraw Rag), The Chimes, Cornell, Cornell Hymn, Cornell Victorious, Crew Song, Dreaming, 1875, Evening Song, Everybody Works But Davy, Faculty, Fight For Cornell, Give My Regards To Davy; Hail Thou In Majesty, Cornell; The Hill, In the Red and the White, The Isle Of Delight, Lingering, Loyal Daughters Of Cornell; March On, Cornell; One, Two, Three, Four; The Poughkeepsie Course, Rowing Song, The Senior Dirge; Senior, Junior, Soph'more, Frosh; Song For Cornell, Song of the Classes, Song of the Classes (Women's Version), Tales of Old Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled and Edited by Thomas A. Sokol. Published by the Cornell University Glee Club. Includes multiple voice part arrangements of the songs.Includes: Alma Mater, Alumni Song, The Big Red Team, Carnelian And White, The Chimes, Cornell, Cornell Champions, Cornell Changes, Cornell Hymn, Cornell Victorious, Crew Song, 1875, Evening Song, Fight For Cornell, Gaudeamus Igitur, Give My Regards To Davy, Hail Thou In Majesty, Cornell, The Hill, In The Red And The White, March On, Cornell, My Old Cornell, New Cornell Fight Song, Song FOr Cornell, Song Of The Classes, Strike Up A Song To Cornell, Tales Of Old Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collected and printed for their fifty-fifth reunion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lion's Rock, 316 East 77th Street, New York, NY Includes: Selections reproduced from Songs of Cornell 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An Alumni Affairs publication sponsored by the Cornell Association of Class Officers.Includes: Alma Mater, Alumni Song, The Big Red Team, Carnelian And White, Cornell, Cornell Hymn, Cornell Victorious, Crew Song, 1875, Evening Song, Fight For Cornell, Give My Regards To Davy, Hail Thou In Majesty, Cornell, The Hill, In The Red And The White, March On, Cornell, My Old Cornell, Song For Cornell, Song Of The Classes, Strike Up A Song To Cornell.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Miscellaneous Lyric Sheets and Music Related Items

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lyrics by Archibald C. Weeks '72.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No music, just lyrics. From a Tuesday night, November 27 rally at Bailey Hall.Includes: The Big Red Team, Fight for Cornell, Carnelian and White, Cornell Victorious.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: America, Our Empire State, Let The Rest Of The World Go By, Long, Long Trail, Auld Lang Syne, Swanee River, America The Beautiful, Sailing, The Mummy Song, Sweet Adeline, 'Till We Eat Again, Pack Up Your Troubles In Your Old Kit Bag, Levee Song, School Days, Smiles, The Old Grey Mare, 'Round Her Neck She Wears A Yeller Ribbon, Round: - When You Meet A Donkey, Jingle Bells, Battle Hymn Of The Republic, Yawnin In The Mawnin, We're Here For Fun, Our Boys Will Shine, The Old Apple Pipe, The Damper Song, Carry Me Back To Old Virginny, Down In De Cornfield, Annie Laurie, Maggie, Dunderback, There Ain't No Flies On Us, In The Good Old Summer Time, The More We Are Together, Pussy Willow Song, Old MacDonald Had A Far, Our Cow, Style All The While, Chew-Chew, Paving, Moonlight And Roses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to be published by the Cornell Glee Club. No music, just lyrics. Includes: The Chimes, Cornell, Alma Mater, The Winter, Hark; I Hear A Voice, I love My Love, Moustache, The Old Cabin Home, Smoking Song, Bingo, All Together, Landlord, Fill Your Flowing Bowl, Solomon Levi, Rumsty Ho!, What Am The Song What I Do Sing, Hard Up.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No music, just lyrics.Also includes: There's Music In The Air, Landlord, Fill Your Flowing Bowl, 'Twas Oft The Blue Canaries, I've A Jolly Sixpence, 'Tis A Way We Have At Cornell, Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various pieces all titled Cornell Songs, none have music, just lyrics.Songs include: Alma Mater, Cornell, Don't Send My Boy to Harvard, The Red and the White, Song for Cornell, Song of the Classes, Song of the Classes (Women's Version), Fight for Cornell, Senior Dirge, Cornell Victorious, Give My Regards to Davy, Carnelian and White, Alumni Song, Evening Song, Crew Song, Reunion Song (Women), Loyal Daughters of Cornell, Bustonian Chorus, Poughkeepsie Course,, Eli, March on Cornell!, Whiffenpoof Song, Lord Geoggrey Amherst, Keep the Home Fires Burning, There's a Long Long Trail A'winding, Chiming Bells, The Chimes, In Bohemia Hall, On the Road to Mandalay, Dink a High Ball, Eveline, When You Wore a Tulip, Abdulla Bulbul Ameer, There is a Tavern in the Town, Mandy Lee, Love's Old Sweet Song, Missouri Waltz, Sweet and Low, Steal Away, Nobody Knows, Swing Low! Sweet Chariot!, Old Black Joe, Beside a Belgian Waterfall, Clementine, The Titanic, Silver Dollar, Pack Up Your Troubles in Your Old Kit Bag, Juanita, Smiles, My Wild Irish Rose, Jacob's Ladder, Jeanie With The Light Brown Hair, Alexander's Ragtime Band, Anchors Aweigh, The Caisson Song, The Marines' Hymn, Army Air Corps Song, It's A Long Way To Tipperary, Over There, I Lost My Heart To A Beta, Shortnin' Bread, Oh Susanna, The Man On The Flying Trapeze, Stein Song, Far Away In The South, The Pope, Mermaid, O, Bring The Wagon, Home, John, Good Night, My Love, Auld Lang Syne, 1875, The Chimes, Kindling-wood, Drinking-song, In The Evening By The Moonlight, I Used To Know A Little Yellow Girl, I've Benn Working On The Levee, Bring the Wagon Home, We Meet Again To-night.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Long, Short, Locomotive, Marching Or Double, Siren

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by the Lehigh Valley Railroad.Includes: Cornell, Evening Song, Stop, Stop, Stop, By The Light Of The Silvery Moon, Moonlight In Jungle Land, Mary Jones, Bring The Wagon Home John, Come Josephine In My Flying Machine, Song Of The Classes, The Big Red Team, Alma Mater, Cornell Rowing Song, Kiss Me, Down In Louisiana, Top Of The Morning To You, For I Love It, Put Your Arms Around Me, Honey, Crew Song, Come Be My Lindy Jane.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hymns sung at the June 13, 1875 opening of the chapel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Cornell, song to the Air Dearest Mae, Bull Dog, Smoking Song, Alma Mater, Here's To Old Cornell, Parting Ode by Robert H. Treman, sung to Auld Lang Syne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lyrics by William Nelson Smith '90. No music, just lyrics. Dedicated to Sophomores in General and Engineers in Particular.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably a song book for the Class of 1891. No music, just lyrics. Includes Class Song, Vive 'L Amour, The Pope, Rosalie, Jingle Bells, Solomon Levi, The Chimes, The Owl and the Pussy-Cat, Alma Mater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Susan E. Linn Sage. Published after being found in her papers after her death in 1885. reverse has note "Presented to E. F. Curtis by Miss Linn, October 1898.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: America, Cornell, Evening Song, The Chimes, Cornell Rowing Song, Alma Mater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Alma Mater, Smoking Song by Judge F. M. Finch, Evening Song, sung to the air Maryland, Cornell, sung to the air Dearest Mae, The Chimes, sung to the air Tramp, Tramp, Tramp, Cornell Rowing Song, America.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Alma Mater, Smoking song by Judge F. M. Finch, Evening Song, Cornell, The Chimes, Cornell Rowing Song, America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composed by Burt G. Wilder. Second (Alumni) Edition produced by the Cornell -Co-operative society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No music, lyrics only, on a small card. Arhur H. Crist '90 publisher. Sung to the air I Want To Go Back To Michigan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lyrics by Albert William Smith '78. Music by James T. Quarles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell Daily Sun article explaining the origins of the song composed by Roland Franklin Andrews '00.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lyrics by John D. Guthrie '39. Sung to the air Home on the Range. From a Cornell Forestry student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music sheets for many instruments in the band. Songs include: Alma Mater, March On Cornell, Cornell Victorious, Carnelian And White, Fight For Cornell, The Big Red Team, Evening Song.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article and reprint of article by Romeyn Berry discussing the proper way to sing the Zinck's line in Give My Regards to Davy. Also includes information about the Glee Club drinking at Zinck's and other stories about the bar and bar owner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music sheets for many instruments in the band.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music sheets for many instruments in the band.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various instrument parts for the songs Big Red Team, Cornell Victorious, Fight For Cornell, March On, My Old Cornell
